Thus, although technically forming *near* or *within* the initial segment of the internal carotid artery, it starts within the wall of what we refer to medically as **the bifurcating portion of the common carotid artery**, making **Option A (Common carotid artery)** more precise as per standard anatomical teaching.

$\boxed{\text{A}}$

Therefore, its anatomical location is best described as within or at the origin of the common carotid artery.
